title: Claritev Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel for More Information About Potentially Recovering Their Losses
content:
SAN DIEGO, Feb.  24, 2026  (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Johnson Fistel, PLLP is investigating potential claims on behalf of investors of Claritev Corporation (NYSE: CTEV). The investigation focuses on Claritev’s executive officers and whether investor losses may be recovered under federal securities laws.

What if I purchased Claritev securities?

If you purchased Claritev securities and suffered losses on your investment, join our investigation now: Click here to join the investigation.

Or for more information, contact Jim Baker at jimb@johnsonfistel.com or (619) 814-4471.

There is no cost or obligation to you.

Background of the investigation

On February 23, 2026, after the market closed, Claritev reported its fourth quarter and full-year 2025 financial results. The Company disclosed that adjusted EBITDA margin for the fourth quarter was 61.4% and that full-year adjusted EBITDA margin was 62.4%. Claritev also provided 2026 revenue growth guidance of approximately 2% to 4%.

Following this disclosure, Claritev’s shares declined approximately 45%.

In light of this disclosure, Johnson Fistel is investigating whether Claritev complied with the federal securities laws. If you suffered losses from your investment in Claritev stock, contact Johnson Fistel.

About Johnson Fistel, PLLP | Securities Fraud & Investor Rights

Johnson Fistel, PLLP is a nationally recognized shareholder-rights law firm with offices in California, New York, Georgia, Idaho, and Colorado. The firm represents individual and institutional investors in shareholder derivative and securities class action lawsuits and also assists foreign investors who purchased shares on U.S. exchanges. To learn more, visit www.johnsonfistel.com.

Achievements

In 2024, Johnson Fistel was ranked among the Top 10 Plaintiff Law Firms by ISS Securities Class Action Services. This recognition reflects the firm’s effectiveness in advocating for investors, having recovered approximately $90,725,000 for aggrieved clients in cases where it served as lead or co-lead counsel. This marks the eighth time the firm has been recognized as a top plaintiffs’ securities law firm in the United States, based on the total dollar value of final recoveries.

Attorney advertising.
Past results do not guarantee future outcomes.
Services may be performed by attorneys in any of our offices.
Johnson Fistel, PLLP has paid for the dissemination of this promotional communication, and Frank J. Johnson is the attorney responsible for its content.

Contact

Johnson Fistel, PLLP
501 W. Broadway, Suite 800
San Diego, CA 92101
James Baker, Investor Relations – or – Frank J. Johnson, Esq.
(619) 814-4471 | jimb@johnsonfistel.com | fjohnson@johnsonfistel.com

title: Claritev outlines 2026 revenue guidance of $980M–$1B as digital transformation accelerates
content:
Earnings Call Insights: Claritev Corporation (CTEV) Q4 2025

MANAGEMENT VIEW

 	* Travis Dalton, CEO, President & Chairman, stated the company has “returned to profitable growth sooner than expected,” emphasizing that Claritev’s long-term Vision 2030 is focused on building a disciplined healthcare technology company delivering sustainable, profitable growth. He highlighted that “our strategy is working,” with a focus on transparency and affordability in healthcare, supported by recent client renewals and sales growth. 
 	* Dalton explained, “We achieved 6.2% revenue growth in Q4 year-over-year, continuing to demonstrate not only durability, but that our vertical market strategy is working against our product roadmap.” He also announced record Q4 bookings of $23 million and the acquisition of 30 new logos in 2025.
 	* Dalton introduced the new AI-driven Network Builder solution, describing it as a tool that enables the creation of tailored provider networks “in minutes with a few keystrokes, replacing what used to be a lengthy, manual process.” He also noted international expansion, stating, “We are expanding our footprint internationally in the Middle East, signing two additional clients that will create momentum for growth in 2026.”
 	* Doug Garis, Executive VP & CFO, stated, “Total revenue in Q4 was $246.6 million, up 6.2% year-over-year. Growth in Q4 came from both our core business and expansion areas.” He noted that adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $151.3 million at a 61.4% margin, and levered free cash flow was $36.4 million. He further indicated a shift of some previously capitalized costs to OpEx and highlighted a focus on “driving revenue growth at good margins, combined with a keen focus on improving free cash flow.”

OUTLOOK

 	* Garis presented 2026 revenue guidance of $980 million to $1 billion, representing 2% to 4% growth over 2025. Excluding $18 million in one-time revenue from 2025, he said, “we are modeling 4% to 6% growth this year.”
 	* He provided adjusted EBITDA guidance of $605 million to $615 million, with margins of 61% to 62%. Garis outlined that “we are projecting free cash flow of $0 million to $10 million this year.”
 	* Management expects low single digit growth in Q1 and modest sequential growth in Q2, with revenue growth picking up to 3% to 5% in the second half of the year as new ACV ramps.

FINANCIAL RESULTS

 	* For Q4 2025, Claritev reported total revenue of $246.6 million, adjusted EBITDA of $151.3 million (61.4% margin), and levered free cash flow of $36.4 million. Year-end cash was reported as $28 million, with $17 million unrestricted. Net leverage at year-end stood at 7.7x.
 	* Full year 2025 revenue was $965.4 million, with adjusted EBITDA of $602.6 million. Levered free cash flow for the year was a use of $12.3 million.
 	* Q4 included a $5 million one-time revenue benefit in the P&C business, part of a total $18 million non-recurring revenue for 2025. Garis noted, “This will have a zero-dollar cash flow impact this year as we expect total capital investments to remain consistent.”

Q&A

	* NOT_APPLICABLE: The call was cut short due to technical difficulties before the Q&A session could be conducted, and management directed participants to submit questions via email or phone.

SENTIMENT ANALYSIS

 	* Analysts’ sentiment cannot be assessed for Q4 due to the absence of a Q&A session from technical disruptions.
 	* Management maintained a confident and positive tone throughout, with Dalton stating, “We are well into our digital transformation that will create a stronger and more durable operating and technology platform for the long haul.” Garis described the company as “on the Way Up with a growing top and bottom line, and an emerging business model built for durable growth.”
 	* Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remains confident, emphasizing operational execution, digital transformation, and AI leadership.

QUARTER-OVER-QUARTER COMPARISON

 	* Q4 revenue was $246.6 million, similar to Q3’s $246 million, while adjusted EBITDA decreased from $155.1 million (63.1% margin) in Q3 to $151.3 million (61.4% margin) in Q4.
 	* Management reiterated 2026 revenue guidance of $980 million to $1 billion, compared to raised 2025 guidance of 2.8% to 3.2% growth given in Q3.
 	* Strategic focus has shifted from client renewals and pipeline expansion in Q3 to international expansion, product innovation (e.g., Network Builder), and operational transformation in Q4.
 	* The tone remains positive but with a greater emphasis on innovation, AI, and operational efficiency as key differentiators for 2026.
 	* Analysts’ perspectives from Q3 focused on margin stability, ACV conversion, and NSA product economics; such feedback is absent in Q4 due to the lack of Q&A.

RISKS AND CONCERNS

 	* Dalton cited rising costs, high-cost claims, a fluid regulatory environment, and industry fragmentation as ongoing challenges.
 	* Garis highlighted the transition of capitalized costs to OpEx as part of the cloud shift, which will impact EBITDA but not cash flow, and noted the non-recurring revenue that will not repeat in 2026.
 	* Key market variables for 2026 projections include out-of-network claims volume, medical inflation, and claims mix, with management modeling conservatively for these factors.

FINAL TAKEAWAY

Claritev’s management underscored a successful transition to profitable growth in 2025, driven by client renewals, record bookings, and new solutions like Network Builder. The company outlined expectations for 2026 with revenue guidance of $980 million to $1 billion, supported by continued digital transformation, AI integration, and international expansion. Operational discipline, margin focus, and free cash flow improvement remain at the forefront as Claritev positions itself for durable growth amid evolving healthcare industry dynamics.

title: Quant snapshot: Amerigo, Allianz lead strong buys as Ashford Hospitality, Hudson Pacific lag
content:
[Floating Metrics - Abstract Visualization of Growth and Momentum]
J Studios

The latest quant ratings highlight a sharp divergence across sectors, with Materials and Financials dominating the top rankings, while Real Estate and Health Care feature among the weakest-scoring names.

Materials names led the table, with copper producer Amerigo Resources (ARREF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ARREF]) earning the highest overall score at 4.98. Gold miner Aura Minerals (AUGO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AUGO]) followed closely at 4.94, signaling strong factor momentum and fundamentals within the metals and mining space. European insurance giants Allianz SE (ALIZY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ALIZY]) and AXA SA (AXAHY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AXAHY]) both posted robust scores above 4.9, reflecting earnings strength and valuation support in the multi-line insurance segment.

Real estate names occupied the bottom of the rankings, with hotel REIT Ashford Hospitality Trust (AHT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AHT]) posting the lowest overall score at 1.03. Office-focused Hudson Pacific Properties (HPP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HPP]) also screened poorly, reflecting sector-wide headwinds tied to leverage, occupancy trends, and earnings pressure. Biotech and pharmaceutical stocks such as Akebia Therapeutics (AKBA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AKBA]) and Corcept Therapeutics (CORT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CORT]) clustered near the bottom, reflecting weaker growth, valuation, or momentum factor readings.

High-profile companies set to report earnings this week include Nvidia (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA]), Salesforce (CRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRM]), The Home Depot (HD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HD]), and Baidu (BIDU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BIDU]). All four carry Hold ratings, with scores of with scores of 3.48 [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA/ratings/quant-ratings], 3.38 [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRM/ratings/quant-ratings], 3.15 [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HD/ratings/quant-ratings] and 3.35 [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BIDU/ratings/quant-ratings] repectively

Seeking Alpha’s Quant system ranks stocks based on their relative performance across critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. Ratings are given on a scale from 1 to 5, with any score of 3.5 or above considered a bullish rating, while 2.5 or below indicates a bearish assessment.
